lines: +summary: "Support full-text search functions for unmapped fields with `LOAD` and return `null` for partially unmapped fields that cannot be implicitly converted"
@@ -28,10 +28,19 @@ command to create computed columns instead.
[`PROMQL`](/reference/query-languages/esql/commands/promql.md) queries have their own specific semantics for unmapped fields.
-- `FORK`, `LOOKUP JOIN`, subqueries, views, and full-text search functions are not yet supported anywhere in the query.
+- `FORK`, `LOOKUP JOIN`, subqueries, and views are not yet supported anywhere in the query.
- Referencing subfields of `flattened` parents is not supported.
-- Referencing partially unmapped non-keyword fields must be inside a cast or a conversion function (e.g. `::TYPE` or `TO_TYPE`),
+  - Full-text search functions are not supported anywhere in the query. {applies_to}`stack: preview =9.4`
+- Partially unmapped non-`keyword` fields can be used in expressions. If the field is mapped to a single type and there's an
+  available conversion from `keyword` to that type, the implicit conversion is applied. If there's no available conversion,
+  and an explicit one has not been provided by the user, values remain typed where mapped and are `null` for rows from
+  indices where the field is unmapped. {applies_to}`stack: preview 9.5`
+  - Partially unmapped non-`keyword` fields must be referenced inside a cast or conversion function (e.g. `::TYPE` or `TO_TYPE`),

-  "description" : "Determines how unmapped fields are treated. Possible values are:

- `DEFAULT` : Standard ESQL queries fail when referencing unmapped fields.
- `NULLIFY` : Treats unmapped fields as null values.
- `LOAD` : Loads unmapped fields from the stored [`_source`](https://www.elastic.co/docs/reference/elasticsearch/mapping-reference/mapping-source-field)
with type `keyword`. Or nullifies them if absent from `_source`. {applies_to}`stack: preview 9.4`

An `unmapped field` is a field referenced in a query that does not exist in the mapping of the index being queried.
When querying multiple indices, a field is considered `partially unmapped` if it exists in the mapping of some
indices but not others.

Unmapped fields are different from
[runtime fields](docs-content://manage-data/data-store/mapping/runtime-fields.md).
Runtime fields are computed fields defined in the index
mapping that {{esql}} treats like regular mapped fields.
You cannot define new runtime fields at search time in
{{esql}}, but you can use the
[`EVAL`](https://www.elastic.co/docs/reference/query-languages/esql/commands/eval)
command to create computed columns instead.

[`PROMQL`](https://www.elastic.co/docs/reference/query-languages/esql/commands/promql) queries have their own specific semantics for unmapped fields.

Special notes about the `LOAD` option:
- `FORK`, `LOOKUP JOIN`, subqueries, views, and full-text search functions are not yet supported anywhere in the query.
- Referencing subfields of `flattened` parents is not supported.
- Referencing partially unmapped non-keyword fields must be inside a cast or a conversion function (e.g. `::TYPE` or `TO_TYPE`),
unless referenced in a `KEEP` or `DROP`.
"

+  "description" : "Determines how unmapped fields are treated. Possible values are:

- `DEFAULT` : Standard ESQL queries fail when referencing unmapped fields.
- `NULLIFY` : Treats unmapped fields as null values.
- `LOAD` : Loads unmapped fields from the stored [`_source`](https://www.elastic.co/docs/reference/elasticsearch/mapping-reference/mapping-source-field)
with type `keyword`. Or nullifies them if absent from `_source`. {applies_to}`stack: preview 9.4`

An `unmapped field` is a field referenced in a query that does not exist in the mapping of the index being queried.
When querying multiple indices, a field is considered `partially unmapped` if it exists in the mapping of some
indices but not others.

Unmapped fields are different from
[runtime fields](docs-content://manage-data/data-store/mapping/runtime-fields.md).
Runtime fields are computed fields defined in the index
mapping that {{esql}} treats like regular mapped fields.
You cannot define new runtime fields at search time in
{{esql}}, but you can use the
[`EVAL`](https://www.elastic.co/docs/reference/query-languages/esql/commands/eval)
command to create computed columns instead.

[`PROMQL`](https://www.elastic.co/docs/reference/query-languages/esql/commands/promql) queries have their own specific semantics for unmapped fields.

Special notes about the `LOAD` option:
- `FORK`, `LOOKUP JOIN`, subqueries, and views are not yet supported anywhere in the query.
- Referencing subfields of `flattened` parents is not supported.
- [Full-text search functions](https://www.elastic.co/docs/reference/query-languages/esql/functions-operators/search-functions) are supported.
  {applies_to}`stack: preview 9.5`
  - Full-text search functions are not supported anywhere in the query. {applies_to}`stack: preview =9.4`
- [`KNN`](https://www.elastic.co/docs/reference/query-languages/esql/functions-operators/dense-vector-functions/knn) on partially unmapped
  `dense_vector` fields is not yet supported.
- Partially unmapped non-`keyword` fields can be used in expressions. If the field is mapped to a single type and there's an
  available conversion from `keyword` to that type, the implicit conversion is applied. If there's no available conversion,
  and an explicit one has not been provided by the user, values remain typed where mapped and are `null` for rows from
  indices where the field is unmapped. {applies_to}`stack: preview 9.5`
  - Partially unmapped non-`keyword` fields must be referenced inside a cast or conversion function (e.g. `::TYPE` or `TO_TYPE`),
    unless referenced in `KEEP` or `DROP`. {applies_to}`stack: preview =9.4`
"
+        // FORK is not supported with unmapped_fields="load", see https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/142033
"Tests using subqueries are skipped since nested fork/subquery is not supported yet",
+2378            | [Carol Faulkner, Holly Byers Ochoa, Lucretia Mott]
+| WHERE QSTR("txt: Faulkner") OR txt IS NULL
+| WHERE KQL("txt: Faulkner") OR txt IS NULL
+| WHERE MATCH(txt, "Faulkner") OR txt IS NULL
+| WHERE txt:"Faulkner" OR txt IS NULL
+# A dense_vector PUNK should null-fill where unmapped, but the KEYWORD->dense_vector auto-cast hex-decodes the _source array and fails.
+# KNN over a partially unmapped dense_vector should null-fill where unmapped, but instead errors that the field does not exist.
+// Single-type partially-unmapped non-keyword fields (PUNKs) with no KEYWORD converter (e.g. aggregate_metric_double),
+// used WITHOUT an explicit cast. Under unmapped_fields="load" they fall back to their mapped type and nullify the
+// unmapped rows, matching the default (no-load) behavior.
+// Auto-cast PUNKs (convertible types), casted PUNKs, and multi-type union conflicts live in unmapped-type-conflicts.csv-spec.
+// network.eth0.tx is aggregate_metric_double in k8s-downsampled and unmapped in k8s_unmapped.
+// Under load mode, function application should keep mapped rows and nullify unmapped ones.
+// network.eth0.tx is aggregate_metric_double in k8s-downsampled, unmapped in k8s_unmapped (present in _source as an integer),
+// and entirely absent in k8s_nonexistent. Under load, a global aggregate over this single-type PUNK keeps the mapped values;
+// the unmapped and non-existent rows contribute null to the metric aggregates while still counting toward COUNT(*).
+| STATS avg_tx = AVG(network.eth0.tx), sum_tx = SUM(network.eth0.tx), tx_count = COUNT(network.eth0.tx), rows = COUNT(*)
+| STATS avg_tx = AVG(network.eth0.tx), sum_tx = SUM(network.eth0.tx), tx_count = COUNT(network.eth0.tx), rows = COUNT(*) BY _index
+// A single-type aggregate_metric_double PUNK has no KEYWORD converter, so under load it falls back to its mapped type and may be renamed.
+// The renamed field behaves exactly as the default setting: real aggregates where mapped, null metrics and 0 COUNT where unmapped.
+| STATS avg_tx = AVG(renamed_tx), sum_tx = SUM(renamed_tx), tx_count = COUNT(renamed_tx), rows = COUNT(*)
+// Same rename grouped by _index: matches aggregateMetricDoubleUnmappedStatsNoCastByIndex, confirming the rename is transparent.
+| STATS avg_tx = AVG(renamed_tx), sum_tx = SUM(renamed_tx), tx_count = COUNT(renamed_tx), rows = COUNT(*) BY _index
-// Verify that a partially-mapped TEXT field is not automatically loaded from _source for the unmapped field index.
-// employees_no_gender doesn't have the gender field in its mappings, but it does have it in its _source, while
-// employees_gender_text has gender mapped as a TEXT field. Don't load gender from _source for employees_no_gender.
+// Verify that a partially-mapped TEXT field is not implicitly converted from KEYWORD under LOAD.
+// employees_no_gender doesn't have the gender field in its mappings, so its values are null.
+// employees_gender_text has gender mapped as a TEXT field.
+         * Null-fallback under {@code unmapped_fields="load"}: full-text search functions are supported, and single-type partially
+         * unmapped non-keyword fields (PUNKs) fall back to their mapped type, nullifying the unmapped rows -- matching the default
+         * (no-load) behavior -- instead of being rejected. This also lets such fields be renamed and used in expressions.
* Bugfix: {@code IndexResolver.mergedMappings} crashed with {@code UnsupportedOperationException} when a keyword
* field with multi-fields (e.g. {@code my_field.analyzed}) was partially unmapped across the queried indices and
@@ -482,12 +483,8 @@ private static void mappingAsAttributes(List<Attribute> list, Source source, Str
name = parentName == null ? name : parentName + "." + name;
-                // due to a bug also copy the field since the Attribute hierarchy extracts the data type
-                // directly even if the data type is passed explicitly
-                    t = new EsField(t.getName(), type, t.getProperties(), t.isAggregatable(), t.isAlias(), t.getTimeSeriesFieldType());
// Resolve surrogates immediately, since expressions stored in UnionTypeEsField are serialized
// to data nodes, and SurrogateExpressions cannot be serialized.
+                                // At the moment this path is exercised by TO_DEGREES/TO_RADIANS for single-type PUNKs under LOAD.
+                                // Function cannot consume keyword, so keep mapped branches and nullify unmapped ones. See #150378.
throw new IllegalStateException("Unexpected potentially unmapped expression for [" + fa.fieldName() + "]");
-         * Check if all the original types in the {@code UnionTypeEsField} are supported by the convert function. If the field is partially
-         * unmapped, we additionally check if the convert function accept KEYWORD.
-         * @param supportedTypes The types supported by the convert function
+         * Check if all the original mapped types in the {@code UnionTypeEsField} are supported by the convert function.
+         * If the field is partially unmapped and the function cannot consume {@code KEYWORD}, the unmapped branches are nullified
-        private static boolean canConvertOriginalTypes(UnionTypeEsField unionTypeEsField, Set<DataType> supportedTypes) {
